place: Massapequa Park New York, New York

facts.text: The Historical Society of the Massapequas preserves Massapequa Park’s heritage through archives, events, and a historic complex featuring Old Grace Church.
Brady and Colleran Parks honor two village founders whose leadership shaped Massapequa Park’s early development.
Massapequa Park’s Massapequa Park station on the LIRR’s Babylon Branch keeps the village connected to New York City.
In 1929, Massapequa Park hosted the Fitzmaurice Flying Field, named after a pioneer who helped fly across the Atlantic.
The Delancey Floyd-Jones Free Library, celebrated in a 125‑year‑old article, remains a cornerstone of local learning.
German immigrants from Brooklyn settled in what became Massapequa Park, calling the area Wurtenberg in the 19th century.
